mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a walk-forward t-test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of acceleration periods, or the signal reverses direction during inflationary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 205.0, elevated inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts anticipatory monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in >65% of months above the threshold, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative returns in >50% of observation windows, or the interaction term loses predictive power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising consumer prices indicates supply-constrained stagflation, compressing profit margins and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns during supply shocks, or 5-day returns are positive in >55% of stagflationary periods.
